comments.author: Alexander Terp
comments.body:
Inaccuracy in card body + a refinement to add:

**Card body inaccuracy:** the intro paragraph says `checkAssignAgainstDeclared, and checkIndexedAssignTarget are now Error` - but `type_check.go:2104` still defaults to `IssueHint` and only escalates to `IssueError` when the value is null. Confirmed via `./bin/radd check` on `bb = "hi"` after `bb: int = 5` - fires as HINT, not ERROR. The body's bullet that mentions checkAssignAgainstDeclared/checkIndexedAssignTarget under the structural-literal fix section is the correct picture.

**Scalar-only refinement (low-hanging fruit within this card):** the hold-back rationale for checkAssignAgainstDeclared and checkArgType cites structural-literal fidelity (str literal vs StrEnum, map literal vs struct, list literal vs tuple). But the bulk of mismatches in real code are plain primitive-vs-primitive (`bb: int = 5; bb = "hi"` or `foo("hi")` for `foo(bar: int)`). These have zero structural-literal nuance and could be promoted today via a guard like:
```
if isNonStructuralPrimitive(valType) && isNonStructuralPrimitive(declared) {
    severity = IssueError
}
```
where isNonStructuralPrimitive returns true for str, int, float, bool, null (and their optional forms). This closes the most common mismatch class without risking the structural false positives the hold-back protects against.

Surfaced during round-2 verification (2026-05-25). Headline reproducer: `bb: int = 5; bb = "hi"` shows up as a HINT in VS Code, not an Error - same script gates correctly under the proposed scalar-only refinement.

comments.author: Alexander Terp
comments.body:
Done - shipped 2026-05-31 → 2026-06-01 across commits e33fd269, e83aa78d, 95890d84, 9d986d86. The implementation diverged from this card's sketch:

- Severity promotion landed as a provable-only ("gateable") model rather than a binary flip. A mismatch gates as Error exactly when every possible runtime value is incompatible, and stays a Hint when a union/optional/gradual-container arm might still fit. This dissolves the false-positive-vs-hold-back tension the card was stuck on. All 6 named sites use it (checkArgType/checkAssignAgainstDeclared -> mismatchSeverity; recordReturn/checkIndexedAssignTarget -> typeIsGateable; addOpIssue/addUnaryOpIssue -> opMismatchSeverity; emitNonExhaustiveSwitch was already Error).

- Gap 1 (structural-literal fidelity): solved by rts/check/check_lit.go's recursive check() -> checkListLit()/checkMapLit(), matching list-vs-tuple, map-vs-struct, str-vs-StrEnum. The proposed litMatchesTuple/Struct/StrEnum helpers were NOT built - a single recursive dispatcher replaced that approach.

- Gap 2 (union-narrowing on fallible): solved by maybeStripFallible() stripping the error arm at the call site, so a = parse_int("2"); a + 1 checks clean. A separate opt-in advisory (RAD30011, --strict only) nudges handling the error.

- The comment's scalar-only isNonStructuralPrimitive refinement was NOT implemented and is moot: primitive-vs-primitive mismatches are inherently provable, so the gateable model already gates them as Error.

Snapshots assert [error] across core/testing/snapshots/types/{typing,typing_nested}.snap and rts/check/snapshots/{fallible,lit_check/structural,narrow/str_enum,narrow/typed_local,fn_value/structural_match}.snap (RAD30001: 29 error vs 5 hint, hints reserved for uncertain unions). Also fixed one stale snapshot description in narrow/typed_local.snap that still said "fires a Hint".

creator: Alexander Terp
description:
Commit 1 of verify-notes work landed a partial severity flip: emitNonExhaustiveSwitch, checkAssignAgainstDeclared, and checkIndexedAssignTarget are now Error, but the rest (checkArgType, recordReturn, addOpIssue, addUnaryOpIssue) are still Hint because the static side has real fidelity gaps that would false-positive valid scripts.

Two distinct gap classes blocking full promotion:

**1. Structural-literal fidelity (blocks checkArgType + recordReturn + future tightening of checkAssignAgainstDeclared / checkIndexedAssignTarget):**

The synth layer loses literal shape when collection literals flow into structured typed slots. Concrete cases caught in the smoke test:
- \`fn foo() -> [int, str]: return [1, "2"]\` - list literal synths to \`int|str[]\`, doesn't structurally match the tuple \`[int, str]\`. Runtime accepts; static would reject.
- \`fn foo() -> {"key1": int}: return {"key1": 10}\` - map literal synths to \`{ str: int }\`, doesn't match named-key struct.
- \`fn foo() -> ["foo", "bar"]: return "foo"\` - str literal synths to \`str\`, doesn't match StrEnum (only handled at checkArgType via litMatchesStrEnum, not at recordReturn).
- \`foo({ "outer": { "inner": 42 } })\` against \`foo(x: { "outer": { "inner": int } })\` - same pattern.
- \`foo([helper, helper])\` against \`foo(fns: fn() -> void[])\` - fn-element-in-list case.

**Fix shape:** extend the litMatchesStrEnum AST-peek pattern across literal kinds and call sites. Sketch:
- litMatchesTuple(argNode, expected *TypingTupleT): peek list literal, recursively check element-i against tuple-position-i.
- litMatchesStruct(argNode, expected *TypingStructT): peek map literal, check each named key + recursively check value type.
- Generalize litMatchesStrEnum so all four call sites (checkArgType, recordReturn, checkAssignAgainstDeclared, checkIndexedAssignTarget) can call it uniformly.
- Once bridges exist for all literal kinds × all call sites, promote checkArgType + recordReturn to Error.

**2. Union-narrowing on fallible-result patterns (blocks addOpIssue + addUnaryOpIssue):**

\`parse_int\` returns \`int|error\`. Existing scripts use it terse:
\`\`\`rad
a = parse_int("2")
print(a + 1)
\`\`\`
At runtime: succeeds. Statically: \`int|error + int\` flags as ErrInvalidTypeForOp. Promoting addOpIssue blocks valid scripts that don't error-narrow.

**Fix options (need design discussion):**
- Auto-narrow on assignment from fallible builtins where type_of(value) != "error" is implied by control flow.
- Require explicit narrowing (\`a = parse_int(...) ?? fail "..."\`) - breaking, terser pattern.
- Special-case fallible builtins so the static check checks the non-error variant (heuristic, less principled).

Once a path is picked, promote addOpIssue + addUnaryOpIssue to Error.

**Files in scope:**
- rts/check/type_check.go (call sites + helpers around lines 137-164, 1872-1896, 1918-1976, 2319-2362, 2776-2826)
- New helpers: rts/check/lit_match.go (or extend existing)

**Snapshots to add when complete:** every failing test under core/testing/types/typing/ and core/testing/types/typing_nested/ that currently passes because we held back; plus the parse_X-in-op tests.

**Reference:** verify-notes.md Closed section claimed both fidelity gaps had landed (commits 67fe418f, ab0de49e + litMatchesStrEnum). Only the str-enum case in checkArgType actually landed; the rest of the matrix is the remaining work.
title: Promote type-check Hint diagnostics to Error: structural-literal + union-narrowing fidelity
